mnemonics: Nested TRY: Think Russian Dolls - You Try (TRY) to handle errors layer by layer, just like opening a set of nested dolls.
keyPoints: An outer try block can contain one or more inner try blocks, creating multiple levels of exception handling.
If an inner try block does not have a catch handler for a particular exception, the exception propagates to the next outer try block.
This structure is useful for handling exceptions at different levels of code granularity.
commonMistakes: Overcomplicating code with excessive nesting when a single try-catch would suffice, reducing readability.
Forgetting that an uncaught exception in an inner block will propagate outward, potentially being caught by an unexpected outer handler.
